summary: Integrity-stopped story can still acquire branch/worktree/dev-spawn — 2.1.3 trigger gated on 2.1.4/2.1.5 resolving, not on outcome.
detail: 2.1.4 integrity stop forbids verifier dispatch only; branch/worktree creation is not a 'verification action', so a literal reading lets a stopped story get git mutations with no cleanup path.
evidence: 'Fire asynchronously after 2.1.4/2.1.5 resolve' vs 2.1.4 'skip all further verification actions'
suggested_fix: Outcome-gate the 2.1.3 trigger: fire only when 2.1.4 confirmed contract unchanged (S not in contract_integrity_stops); integrity-stopped story skips 2.1.3 entirely.

summary: Collision check keys on directory existence, not worktree registration — registration/filesystem disagreement states fail into improvisation.
detail: (a) dir exists unregistered → remove fails, add fails; (b) registered but dir deleted → check false, branch -D fails. Launch loop re-runs mid-build after the only prune.
suggested_fix: Prune first in collision path; base check on git worktree list; fallback rm -rf + prune when remove fails on unregistered path.

summary: git branch -D fails unhandled when branch checked out in a worktree at a non-canonical path.
detail: Phase 1 sweep only removes worktree-agent-* orphans; other paths holding story/{slug} block deletion and subsequent creation.
evidence: 'fatal: branch ... is used by worktree at <other-path>'
suggested_fix: Check git worktree list for any entry on story/{S.slug} and force-remove that path before branch delete.

detail: git worktree remove --force refuses locked working trees; failure cascades into creation.
suggested_fix: Use git worktree remove --force --force in the collision path.

summary: Hardcoded line-number refs ('~lines 159-160','~155-165') in instruction text and eval pass criteria will rot.
detail: Any insertion above shifts the reconcile; eval graders land on unrelated text.
suggested_fix: Replace with stable structural anchor ('the RECONCILE ON START action in Phase 1'), line numbers at most parenthetical.

summary: All inserted git commands use repo-root-relative paths with no working-directory anchor stated.
detail: Worktree paths are CWD-sensitive; wrong CWD silently creates worktree at wrong location.
evidence: 'git worktree add .worktrees/story-{S.slug} story/{S.slug}' — no CWD statement
suggested_fix: Add one sentence: relative paths resolve from repo root; Conductor normalizes CWD (git rev-parse --show-toplevel) before git mutations.

summary: Simultaneous frontier launch places N stories' Conductor git mutations in concurrent flight with no serialization instruction.
detail: Parallel branch/worktree-add calls contend on shared .git state ('.lock File exists' class failures); no error handling stated.
evidence: 'launch ALL simultaneously' + 'CREATE STORY BRANCH AND WORKTREE (Conductor-executed...)'
suggested_fix: State the Conductor performs frontier-batch branch/worktree creation serially; concurrency applies to agents, not Conductor git mutations.

summary: Inserted action never states the deliberate post-gate placement choice (Dev Notes required 'state the choice explicitly').
detail: Only '(Conductor-executed, before dev spawn)' present; no rationale for first-action-of-STAGE-1/post-gate placement.
evidence: Rationale paragraph covers fork point only
suggested_fix: Add one line: 'Placement: deliberately first action of STAGE-1, after 2.1.4/2.1.5 gates resolve, so a gate-stopped story never acquires an orphan worktree.'

location: both new eval files (Verification Method; eval-1 Then #4)
summary: Both evals are inspection-only (static text checks) despite AC6 requiring behavioral evals; eval-1 Then #4 asserts runtime state text inspection cannot establish.
detail: Story EDD guide prescribes spawned-subagent behavior observation; git mechanics cheaply exercisable in scratch repo.
evidence: '## Verification Method **Inspection of workflow text.**' vs Then #4 runtime assertion
suggested_fix: Pair inspection with a behavioral leg (scratch-repo git exercise or spawned-subagent simulation); cut or make checkable eval-1 Then #4.

summary: First pass criterion reads as requiring sprint base ref in BOTH commands — strict grader fails the shipped (correct) implementation.
detail: worktree-add correctly omits the sprint ref; criterion grammar attaches base-ref to both.
evidence: 'branch creation and worktree-add commands with sprint/{{sprint_slug}} stated as the base ref'
suggested_fix: Scope base-ref requirement to the branch-creation command only.

location: workflow.md:173 (reconcile end-condition note) vs collision eval premise
summary: Reconcile note claims ALL prior-session story branches/worktrees removed; new eval's premise codifies a survivor (non-in-progress story).
detail: Task 3.2 required wording adjustment if contradicted; not made.
evidence: note: 'all story branches and worktrees from prior sessions are removed' vs eval: 'not caught by reconcile because the story was not in in-progress state'
suggested_fix: Scope the note's claim to in-progress stories; point leftovers to launch-time collision handling.
